title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. moschatus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. moschatus, also known as the musky-flowered narcissus or the muscari, is a subspecies of the wild daffodil that is native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as hypertension and atherosclerosis. The musky-flowered narcissus has been used in herbal preparations to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, and its essential oil is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the musky-flowered narcissus can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically made by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water.
